plugin.name: Maven Frontend Plugin
plugin.description: This Maven plugin lets you install Node/NPM locally for your project, install dependencies with NPM, install dependencies with bower or jspm, run Grunt or gulp tasks, and/or run Karma tests.

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: Bower arguments. Default is "install".

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: Additional environment variables to pass to the build.

parameter.name: installDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for installing node and npm.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skips execution of this mojo.

parameter.name: skipTests
parameter.description: Whether you should skip while running in the test phase (default is false)

parameter.name: testFailureIgnore
parameter.description: Set this to true to ignore a failure during testing. Its use is NOT RECOMMENDED, but quite convenient on occasion.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for running all Node commands. (Usually the directory that contains package.json)

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: Grunt arguments. Default is empty (runs just the "grunt" command).

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: Additional environment variables to pass to the build.

parameter.name: installDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for installing node and npm.

parameter.name: outputdir
parameter.description: The directory where front end files will be output by grunt. If this is set then they will be refreshed so they correctly show as modified in Eclipse.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skips execution of this mojo.

parameter.name: skipTests
parameter.description: Whether you should skip while running in the test phase (default is false)

parameter.name: srcdir
parameter.description: The directory containing front end files that will be processed by grunt. If this is set then files in the directory will be checked for modifications before running grunt.

parameter.name: testFailureIgnore
parameter.description: Set this to true to ignore a failure during testing. Its use is NOT RECOMMENDED, but quite convenient on occasion.

parameter.name: triggerfiles
parameter.description: Files that should be checked for changes, in addition to the srcdir files. {@link #workingDirectory}.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for running all Node commands. (Usually the directory that contains package.json)

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: Grunt arguments. Default is empty (runs just the "grunt" command).

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: Additional environment variables to pass to the build.

parameter.name: installDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for installing node and npm.

parameter.name: outputdir
parameter.description: The directory where front end files will be output by grunt. If this is set then they will be refreshed so they correctly show as modified in Eclipse.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skips execution of this mojo.

parameter.name: skipTests
parameter.description: Whether you should skip while running in the test phase (default is false)

parameter.name: srcdir
parameter.description: The directory containing front end files that will be processed by grunt. If this is set then files in the directory will be checked for modifications before running grunt.

parameter.name: testFailureIgnore
parameter.description: Set this to true to ignore a failure during testing. Its use is NOT RECOMMENDED, but quite convenient on occasion.

parameter.name: triggerfiles
parameter.description: Files that should be checked for changes, in addition to the srcdir files. Defaults to Gruntfile.js in the {@link #workingDirectory}.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for running all Node commands. (Usually the directory that contains package.json)

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: Gulp arguments. Default is empty (runs just the "gulp" command).

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: Additional environment variables to pass to the build.

parameter.name: installDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for installing node and npm.

parameter.name: outputdir
parameter.description: The directory where front end files will be output by gulp. If this is set then they will be refreshed so they correctly show as modified in Eclipse.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skips execution of this mojo.

parameter.name: skipTests
parameter.description: Whether you should skip while running in the test phase (default is false)

parameter.name: srcdir
parameter.description: The directory containing front end files that will be processed by gulp. If this is set then files in the directory will be checked for modifications before running gulp.

parameter.name: testFailureIgnore
parameter.description: Set this to true to ignore a failure during testing. Its use is NOT RECOMMENDED, but quite convenient on occasion.

parameter.name: triggerfiles
parameter.description: Files that should be checked for changes, in addition to the srcdir files. Defaults to gulpfile.js in the {@link #workingDirectory}.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for running all Node commands. (Usually the directory that contains package.json)

parameter.name: downloadRoot
parameter.deprecated: use {@link #nodeDownloadRoot} and {@link #npmDownloadRoot} instead, this configuration will be used only when no {@link #nodeDownloadRoot} or {@link #npmDownloadRoot} is specified.
parameter.description: Where to download Node.js and NPM binaries from.

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: Additional environment variables to pass to the build.

parameter.name: installDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for installing node and npm.

parameter.name: nodeDownloadRoot
parameter.description: Where to download Node.js binary from. Defaults to https://nodejs.org/dist/

parameter.name: nodeVersion
parameter.description: The version of Node.js to install. IMPORTANT! Most Node.js version names start with 'v', for example 'v0.10.18'

parameter.name: npmDownloadRoot
parameter.description: Where to download NPM binary from. Defaults to https://registry.npmjs.org/npm/-/

parameter.name: npmVersion
parameter.description: The version of NPM to install.

parameter.name: serverId
parameter.description: Server Id for download username and password

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skips execution of this mojo.

parameter.name: skipTests
parameter.description: Whether you should skip while running in the test phase (default is false)

parameter.name: testFailureIgnore
parameter.description: Set this to true to ignore a failure during testing. Its use is NOT RECOMMENDED, but quite convenient on occasion.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for running all Node commands. (Usually the directory that contains package.json)

parameter.name: downloadRoot
parameter.deprecated: use {@link #nodeDownloadRoot} and {@link #pnpmDownloadRoot} instead, this configuration will be used only when no {@link #nodeDownloadRoot} or {@link #pnpmDownloadRoot} is specified.
parameter.description: Where to download Node.js and NPM binaries from.

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: Additional environment variables to pass to the build.

parameter.name: installDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for installing node and npm.

parameter.name: nodeDownloadRoot
parameter.description: Where to download Node.js binary from. Defaults to https://nodejs.org/dist/

parameter.name: nodeVersion
parameter.description: The version of Node.js to install. IMPORTANT! Most Node.js version names start with 'v', for example 'v0.10.18'

parameter.name: pnpmDownloadRoot
parameter.description: Where to download NPM binary from. Defaults to https://registry.npmjs.org/npm/-/

parameter.name: pnpmVersion
parameter.description: The version of NPM to install.

parameter.name: serverId
parameter.description: Server Id for download username and password

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skips execution of this mojo.

parameter.name: skipTests
parameter.description: Whether you should skip while running in the test phase (default is false)

parameter.name: testFailureIgnore
parameter.description: Set this to true to ignore a failure during testing. Its use is NOT RECOMMENDED, but quite convenient on occasion.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for running all Node commands. (Usually the directory that contains package.json)

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: Additional environment variables to pass to the build.

parameter.name: installDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for installing node and npm.

parameter.name: nodeDownloadRoot
parameter.description: Where to download Node.js binary from. Defaults to https://nodejs.org/dist/

parameter.name: nodeVersion
parameter.description: The version of Node.js to install. IMPORTANT! Most Node.js version names start with 'v', for example 'v0.10.18'

parameter.name: serverId
parameter.description: Server Id for download username and password

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skips execution of this mojo.

parameter.name: skipTests
parameter.description: Whether you should skip while running in the test phase (default is false)

parameter.name: testFailureIgnore
parameter.description: Set this to true to ignore a failure during testing. Its use is NOT RECOMMENDED, but quite convenient on occasion.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for running all Node commands. (Usually the directory that contains package.json)

parameter.name: yarnDownloadRoot
parameter.description: Where to download Yarn binary from. Defaults to https://github.com/yarnpkg/yarn/releases/download/...

parameter.name: yarnVersion
parameter.description: The version of Yarn to install. IMPORTANT! Most Yarn names start with 'v', for example 'v0.15.0'.

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: JSPM arguments. Default is "install".

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: Additional environment variables to pass to the build.

parameter.name: installDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for installing node and npm.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skips execution of this mojo.

parameter.name: skipTests
parameter.description: Whether you should skip while running in the test phase (default is false)

parameter.name: testFailureIgnore
parameter.description: Set this to true to ignore a failure during testing. Its use is NOT RECOMMENDED, but quite convenient on occasion.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for running all Node commands. (Usually the directory that contains package.json)

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: Additional environment variables to pass to the build.

parameter.name: installDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for installing node and npm.

parameter.name: karmaConfPath
parameter.description: Path to your karma configuration file, relative to the working directory (default is "karma.conf.js")

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skips execution of this mojo.

parameter.name: skipTests
parameter.description: Whether you should skip while running in the test phase (default is false)

parameter.name: testFailureIgnore
parameter.description: Set this to true to ignore a failure during testing. Its use is NOT RECOMMENDED, but quite convenient on occasion.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for running all Node commands. (Usually the directory that contains package.json)

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: npm arguments. Default is "install".

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: Additional environment variables to pass to the build.

parameter.name: installDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for installing node and npm.

parameter.name: npmRegistryURL
parameter.description: Registry override, passed as the registry option during npm install if set.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skips execution of this mojo.

parameter.name: skipTests
parameter.description: Whether you should skip while running in the test phase (default is false)

parameter.name: testFailureIgnore
parameter.description: Set this to true to ignore a failure during testing. Its use is NOT RECOMMENDED, but quite convenient on occasion.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for running all Node commands. (Usually the directory that contains package.json)

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: npm arguments. Default is "install".

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: Additional environment variables to pass to the build.

parameter.name: installDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for installing node and npm.

parameter.name: npmRegistryURL
parameter.description: Registry override, passed as the registry option during npm install if set.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skips execution of this mojo.

parameter.name: skipTests
parameter.description: Whether you should skip while running in the test phase (default is false)

parameter.name: testFailureIgnore
parameter.description: Set this to true to ignore a failure during testing. Its use is NOT RECOMMENDED, but quite convenient on occasion.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for running all Node commands. (Usually the directory that contains package.json)

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: pnpm arguments. Default is "install".

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: Additional environment variables to pass to the build.

parameter.name: installDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for installing node and npm.

parameter.name: pnpmRegistryURL
parameter.description: Registry override, passed as the registry option during npm install if set.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skips execution of this mojo.

parameter.name: skipTests
parameter.description: Whether you should skip while running in the test phase (default is false)

parameter.name: testFailureIgnore
parameter.description: Set this to true to ignore a failure during testing. Its use is NOT RECOMMENDED, but quite convenient on occasion.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for running all Node commands. (Usually the directory that contains package.json)

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: Webpack arguments. Default is empty (runs just the "webpack" command).

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: Additional environment variables to pass to the build.

parameter.name: installDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for installing node and npm.

parameter.name: outputdir
parameter.description: The directory where front end files will be output by webpack. If this is set then they will be refreshed so they correctly show as modified in Eclipse.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skips execution of this mojo.

parameter.name: skipTests
parameter.description: Whether you should skip while running in the test phase (default is false)

parameter.name: srcdir
parameter.description: The directory containing front end files that will be processed by webpack. If this is set then files in the directory will be checked for modifications before running webpack.

parameter.name: testFailureIgnore
parameter.description: Set this to true to ignore a failure during testing. Its use is NOT RECOMMENDED, but quite convenient on occasion.

parameter.name: triggerfiles
parameter.description: Files that should be checked for changes, in addition to the srcdir files. Defaults to webpack.config.js in the {@link #workingDirectory}.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for running all Node commands. (Usually the directory that contains package.json)

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: npm arguments. Default is "install".

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: Additional environment variables to pass to the build.

parameter.name: installDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for installing node and npm.

parameter.name: npmRegistryURL
parameter.description: Registry override, passed as the registry option during npm install if set.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skips execution of this mojo.

parameter.name: skipTests
parameter.description: Whether you should skip while running in the test phase (default is false)

parameter.name: testFailureIgnore
parameter.description: Set this to true to ignore a failure during testing. Its use is NOT RECOMMENDED, but quite convenient on occasion.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: The base directory for running all Node commands. (Usually the directory that contains package.json)
